Uses of Interface
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.ArrayVerifier

Packages that use ArrayVerifier
Package
Description
com.github.cowwoc.requirements
The Requirements, DefaultRequirements endpoints.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Classes used to verify preconditions, postconditions and invariants of core Java types.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension
Interfaces that may be extended but not implemented (because the methods contain a generic return type).
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal
Design guide

  • Uses of ArrayVerifier in com.github.cowwoc.requirements

    Methods in com.github.cowwoc.requirements that return ArrayVerifier
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static ArrayVerifier<boolean[],Boolean>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(boolean[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive boolean array.
    static ArrayVerifier<byte[],Byte>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(byte[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive byte array.
    static ArrayVerifier<char[],Character>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(char[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive char array.
    static ArrayVerifier<double[],Double>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(double[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive double array.
    static ArrayVerifier<float[],Float>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(float[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive float array.
    static ArrayVerifier<int[],Integer>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(int[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive int array.
    static ArrayVerifier<long[],Long>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(long[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive long array.
    static ArrayVerifier<short[],Short>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(short[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a primitive short array.
    static <E> ArrayVerifier<E[],E>
    DefaultRequirements.requireThat(E[] actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of an Object array.

  • Uses of ArrayVerifier in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

    Methods in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java that return ArrayVerifier
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ArrayVerifier<E[],E>
    CollectionVerifier.asArray(Class<E> type)
    Returns a verifier for the actual value as an array.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.asCollection(Consumer<CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,E>> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.asList(Consumer<ListVerifier<List<E>,E>> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.contains(E expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.contains(E expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.containsAll(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.containsAll(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.containsAny(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.containsAny(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.containsExactly(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.containsExactly(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContain(E element)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContain(E element, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> elements)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContainDuplicates()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any duplicate elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.isEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.isNotEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.isSorted(Comparator<E> comparator)
    Ensures that the actual value is sorted.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    ArrayVerifier.length(Consumer<SizeVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.

    DefaultJavaRequirements.requireThat(boolean[] actual, String name)
